new_classes.label_en: still wine of fresh grapes with PGI, in containers > 2 L (CN 2204 22 79)
new_classes.label_de: Wein aus frischen Weintrauben mit g.g.A., in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 l (KN 2204 22 79)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI) as defined in additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers with a nominal volume greater than 2 litres, falling under CN subheading 2204 22 79.

new_classes.label_en: still wine of fresh grapes with PGI in containers > 2 L (CN 2204 29 xx)
new_classes.label_de: Wein aus frischen Weintrauben mit g.g.A. in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 l
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing a protected geographical indication (PGI) as defined in additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, presented in containers with a nominal volume greater than 2 litres.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the two main discriminating criteria for CN 2204 22 79: (1) the wine bears a Protected Geographical Indication (PGI) quality, modelled via the existing ProtectedGeographicalIndicationQuality class, and (2) it is presented in containers greater than 2 litres (LargeContainer). The reference to additional note 6(a) to Chapter 22, which provides the legal definition of PGI for wines, is not directly formalised as an axiom (it is a cross-reference to an external normative source). The distinction between CN 2204 22 79 and CN 2204 22 80 (which covers the same PGI wines in the same container size range but apparently for a different alcohol-strength bracket or dosage category) cannot be fully resolved from the text provided, which groups both codes together in the note without further sub-criteria. Coverage is therefore set to 0.85, reflecting the unresolved 2279/2280 split and the non-formalised cross-reference to note 6(a).
